title: PowerShell Modules
description: Learn to discover, import, remove, install, and update PowerShell modules from the gallery and local system.

tasks.prompt: List all modules currently loaded in the session.
tasks.explanation: Get-Module without -ListAvailable shows imported modules only.

tasks.prompt: List all modules installed on the system (including not yet imported).
tasks.explanation: -ListAvailable scans module paths for installable modules.

tasks.prompt: Import the 'ActiveDirectory' module into the current session.
tasks.hint: Use Import-Module with the module name.
tasks.explanation: Import-Module loads cmdlets and functions from a module into the session.

tasks.prompt: Remove the 'ActiveDirectory' module from the current session.
tasks.hint: Use Remove-Module with the module name.
tasks.explanation: Remove-Module unloads a module without deleting it from disk.

tasks.prompt: Search the PowerShell Gallery for modules matching the name 'Az'.
tasks.explanation: Find-Module queries the online gallery for available packages.

tasks.prompt: Install the 'Az' module for the current user only.
tasks.explanation: -Scope CurrentUser installs modules without requiring administrator rights.

tasks.prompt: List all commands exported by the 'ActiveDirectory' module.
tasks.explanation: Get-Command -Module shows cmdlets, functions, and aliases from a module.

tasks.prompt: Update the installed 'Az' module to the latest version.
tasks.explanation: Update-Module downloads and installs newer versions from the gallery.
